description.task: Implement a simple mock object for a database repository. The mock should record method calls and return preset responses, simulating Mockito's when().thenReturn() behavior.
instructions: Implement save() to record the call as 'save([user id])' and return the user.
Implement deleteById() to record the call as 'deleteById([id])'.
Output should show the two service calls working correctly plus verification data.
description.hint: In Mockito: UserRepository mock = Mockito.mock(UserRepository.class); when(mock.findById(1)).thenReturn(Optional.of(user)); verify(mock, times(1)).findById(1);
description.expectedOutput:
Alice
Unknown
2
findById called: true
findById call count: 2
Call log: [findById(1), findById(99), findAll()]

description.task: Test a service class by verifying it calls the correct repository methods with the correct arguments. Build a verification system that checks interaction contracts.
instructions: Run Main as-is — the implementation is already provided.
Understand how verifyCalledOnceWith() and verifyNeverCalled() work.
Trace the flow: register() -> sendWelcomeEmail() only.
description.hint: In Mockito: verify(mockEmail, times(1)).sendWelcomeEmail('alice@example.com'); verify(mockEmail, never()).sendOrderConfirmation(any(), anyInt());
description.expectedOutput:
Registering user: alice@example.com
Welcome email sent: true
Order confirmation NOT sent: true
Password reset NOT sent: true
All interactions verified: true

description.task: Implement both a Mock (fully controlled fake) and a Spy (wraps real object, can override specific methods) and demonstrate when to use each.
description.context: Mockito.mock() creates a fake that does nothing by default. Mockito.spy() wraps a real object but allows overriding specific methods. Spies are useful when you want to test real behavior but control side effects.
instructions: Run Main as-is — the implementation is complete.
Understand: SpyInventoryService extends the real class and overrides only sendLowStockAlert() to suppress it.
Real business logic (reserveStock, stock tracking) runs normally.
Verify spy captures the alert trigger without printing it.
description.hint: Use Mockito.spy() for objects where you want real behavior but need to stub specific methods. Use Mockito.mock() when you want full control over all return values.
description.expectedOutput:
--- Spy: Uses real logic, suppresses alert side effect ---
Reserved: true
Remaining stock: 2
Alerts suppressed for: [Laptop]

--- Real object: alert fires in console ---
ALERT: Low stock for Mouse
